title: Financial Accounting (FI)
learning_objectives: Understand the purpose of SAP Financial Accounting (FI).
Learn how financial transactions are managed in SAP.
Understand the key components of the FI module.
Identify common SAP FI Transaction Codes.
Learn how FI integrates with other SAP modules.

introduction.title: What is SAP FI?
introduction.content: SAP Financial Accounting (FI) is the module responsible for recording, processing and reporting all financial transactions within an organization. It ensures that financial records are accurate, compliant with accounting standards, and available for business decision-making.

business_purpose.title: Why is SAP FI Used?
business_purpose.content: Every business performs financial transactions such as purchases, sales, payments and receipts. SAP FI records these transactions automatically, generates financial statements, and provides real-time financial information for management.

major_components.name: General Ledger (GL)
major_components.description: Central repository for all financial transactions.

major_components.name: Accounts Payable (AP)
major_components.description: Manages payments made to vendors and suppliers.

major_components.name: Asset Accounting (AA)
major_components.description: Maintains fixed asset records such as machinery and equipment.

integration.purpose: Automatically records accounting entries for purchases.
Creates accounting documents after customer billing.
Shares financial data for cost analysis.

business_example.title: Example
business_example.content: A supplier delivers raw materials to the company. After Goods Receipt is posted in SAP MM, an accounting document is automatically generated in SAP FI. When the invoice is received and payment is made, FI records the complete financial transaction, ensuring accurate accounting.

best_practices: Maintain proper documentation for every financial transaction.
common_mistakes: Posting to the wrong General Ledger account.
